conceptClusters.title: Retrieved semantic document order and inspection contract
exitEvidence: Learner retrieves semantic HTML, complete-document, logical source-order, keyboard-path, and evidence-surface invariants before presentation changes begin.
Learner proves author styling changes presentation without repairing, replacing, or concealing a broken document structure or task path.

conceptClusters.title: CSS source computed output persistence and document boundary
exitEvidence: Learner connects saved CSS source to matched rules, computed values, rendered output, and a reload-stable artifact checkpoint.
Learner classifies content, semantics, presentation, behavior, browser styles, and user preference before choosing whether CSS is the correct layer.

conceptClusters.title: CSS rule anatomy parser recovery application and load evidence
exitEvidence: Learner labels and authors qualified rules, selectors, declaration blocks, declarations, properties, values, terminators, comments, and bounded at-rules while predicting parser recovery.
Learner chooses external, internal, or inline application from reuse and ownership constraints and verifies exact path, response, media type, order, and inspected rule evidence.

conceptClusters.title: Universal type class and identifier selector match sets
exitEvidence: Learner predicts and inspects the exact element set matched by universal, type, reusable class, and unique identifier selectors before relying on appearance.
Learner selects the least-coupled proportional hook for changed markup without accidental universal resets, duplicated identifiers, or a unique identifier on every styled element.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A worked evidence trace connects one complete semantic document to a separately loaded first stylesheet, exact selector matches, computed values, saved source, reload behavior, and unchanged keyboard task order.
activityDesigns.startingState: A complete unstyled service page, stylesheet candidate, browser evidence panels, requirement cards, and five plausible near-miss traces are available without accepted conclusions.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner predicts, inspects, and explains all nine new or retrieved concepts through ten committed evidence decisions before guided construction.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies the learner's committed source, match, computed, load, semantic, order, or persistence claim and contrasts it with one observable browser fact.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Worked guidance exposes one layer at a time: document meaning, complete source, order, evidence surface, stylesheet source, rule anatomy, load, match, computation, and persistence.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss routes to the earliest divergent layer and a parallel changed document or stylesheet trace before the learner can continue.

interactions.title: Predict which requirement belongs in HTML
interactions.learnerAction: Classify changed festival requirements as content, semantic relationship, presentation, or dynamic behavior and predict which ones must remain true before CSS loads.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer classification and unstyled invariants are committed.

evidence.observable: Semantic content and task relationships remain available independently of author presentation.
evidence.changedCase: The visual theme is removed while one heading becomes a service instruction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any visible distinction belongs in markup, while CSS can supply missing meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate each requirement as meaning, presentation, or behavior before selecting a language.

interactions.title: Read complete document ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Read and map doctype, root language, head metadata, title, stylesheet relationship, and body content to locate where document and presentation responsibilities are owned.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete-document ownership map is saved.

evidence.observable: The HTML file remains a complete portable document and the CSS relationship is declared in document metadata.
evidence.changedCase: The document moves into a nested route and receives a different title and language.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A rendered body fragment and an inline style attribute are a complete portable page.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source envelope and metadata consumers separately from body rendering.

interactions.title: Predict order without presentation
interactions.learnerAction: Predict reading and keyboard order with author styles enabled and disabled, then reject any visual treatment that changes task meaning without changing logical source order.
interactions.artifactChange: Source, reading, and keyboard sequence predictions are recorded.

evidence.observable: Meaningful sequence and keyboard navigation remain logical before and after presentation is applied.
evidence.changedCase: A call-to-action is displayed prominently and the preceding explanatory section doubles in length.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A prominent visual position proves correct reading and keyboard order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Linearize source and traverse keyboard focus before judging visual order.

interactions.title: Select the evidence surface
interactions.learnerAction: Match malformed source, parsed structure, missing resource, unmatched rule, computed value, task order, and unsaved edit hypotheses to the evidence surface able to falsify each claim.
interactions.artifactChange: Hypothesis-to-evidence selections become checkpoint evidence.

evidence.observable: Each defect hypothesis is tested on source, validator, DOM, network, rule, computed, keyboard, or persistence evidence rather than appearance alone.
evidence.changedCase: The preview looks unchanged after both an invalid declaration and a missing stylesheet response.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One clean validator result or screenshot proves every layer is correct.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name the claim, choose its consumer, and inspect the earliest observable consequence.

interactions.title: Predict source to computed result
interactions.learnerAction: Predict which saved declaration will match, what computed value the browser will expose, and which rendered property changes without treating a visual difference as complete evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Source, match, computed, and render prediction chain is committed.

evidence.observable: One declaration is traceable through matching and computed evidence to rendered output and saved source.
evidence.changedCase: The property value changes from one supported keyword to another and the page reloads.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Changed pixels prove the intended rule matched and the source was saved.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect rule match, computed property, source file, and reload as separate observations.

interactions.title: Explain browser and user style contributions
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how semantic HTML, browser defaults, user preferences, and author CSS each contribute without claiming author styles own content meaning or every computed value.
interactions.artifactChange: Style-origin boundary explanation is stored.

evidence.observable: Explanation attributes meaning to HTML and presentation contributions to the actual browser, user, and author layers observed.
evidence.changedCase: Author CSS is disabled and a user font-size preference is increased.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Author CSS begins from an empty visual state and controls document semantics.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare unstyled rendering, computed evidence, and semantic source before assigning ownership.

interactions.title: Inspect a complete style rule
interactions.learnerAction: Label selector, qualified rule, declaration block, declaration, property, value, colon, semicolon, braces, whitespace, and comment boundaries in a working stylesheet.
interactions.artifactChange: Rule anatomy labels are attached to the inspected source.

evidence.observable: Every syntax part is named by its actual parsing role and the complete rule remains valid.
evidence.changedCase: A second declaration and a nearby comment are inserted into the block.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A property and value anywhere in the file form a complete globally applied rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace tokens outward from property and value to declaration, block, selector, and complete rule.

interactions.title: Predict bounded parser recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether a malformed selector, missing delimiter, unknown property, invalid value, or unfinished comment discards one declaration, one rule, or a broader source region.
interactions.artifactChange: Parser recovery boundaries are committed before inspection.

evidence.observable: Prediction names the smallest actual parse unit rejected and distinguishes later valid declarations or rules that survive.
evidence.changedCase: The malformed token moves from a property value into the selector prelude.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any CSS syntax error disables the entire stylesheet or is silently repaired as intended.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify whether parser is consuming a selector, block, declaration name, value, or comment.

interactions.title: Inspect stylesheet loading evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect relationship markup, resolved URL, request status, response media type, response body, source order, matched rule, and computed value before concluding an external stylesheet loaded.
interactions.artifactChange: External stylesheet load trace is saved.

evidence.observable: The requested stylesheet resolves, returns usable CSS, appears in inspected rules, and contributes the expected computed value.
evidence.changedCase: The HTML document moves one directory deeper and the server returns HTML at the former stylesheet URL.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Seeing a CSS filename in HTML source proves successful application.
interactions.correctionRoute: Follow metadata relationship through URL resolution, network response, parsed rules, match, and computation.

interactions.title: Explain proportional selector hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Explain exact match sets and coupling tradeoffs for universal, type, reusable class, and unique identifier selectors on changed semantic markup.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector choice and rejected alternatives become evidence.

evidence.observable: Chosen hook matches only intended elements, remains proportional to reuse and identity, and preserves meaningful source structure.
evidence.changedCase: A second event card is inserted and the highlighted venue changes identity.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Universal selectors are harmless defaults and every styled element needs a unique identifier.
interactions.correctionRoute: List intended current and future matches, then choose the least-coupled selector with that match set.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A cultural-festival service gains an original external stylesheet through prediction, small edits, inspection, tests, explanations, saved checkpoints, and exact HTML, loading, rule, and selector evidence.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives a complete semantic festival document, an empty stylesheet file, stakeholder presentation needs, browser evidence tools, and no completed style rules.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner has built, inspected, tested, explained, saved, reloaded, and corrected a maintainable first stylesheet while preserving all prior HTML and keyboard invariants.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback names the observed document, source, parser, request, match, computed, render, order, or persistence fact and gives the smallest next inspection or edit.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Three progressive hints point first to the responsible concept, then the evidence surface or source region, then one bounded syntax form without exposing final source.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed interaction preserves learner work, opens a smaller parallel festival example, then requires a changed retry before returning to the cumulative artifact.

interactions.title: Classify festival change requests
interactions.learnerAction: Sort new festival requests for schedule meaning, emphasis appearance, registration behavior, and user text preferences into the responsible HTML, CSS, script, or user layer.
interactions.artifactChange: Festival requirement ownership map is committed.

evidence.observable: Every request is assigned to the layer that owns its meaning or behavior rather than the layer easiest to edit.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative subtitle becomes required explanatory content and a color request becomes optional preference.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Classes can replace semantic elements whenever the styled result looks equivalent.
interactions.correctionRoute: Disable author styles and ask which requirements must still be expressed and usable.

interactions.title: Audit festival document envelope
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the festival doctype, root language, character encoding, viewport metadata, unique title, stylesheet link location, and body landmarks before adding presentation.
interactions.artifactChange: Pre-style complete-document audit is saved.

evidence.observable: Document is complete, metadata is in the head, visible service content is in the body, and relationship ownership is explicit.
evidence.changedCase: Festival language and route basename change before the stylesheet exists.

interactions.feedbackTarget: The browser can infer every missing envelope part, so only visible body markup matters.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare serialized source, parsed document, title consumer, language scope, and validation output.

interactions.title: Test unstyled task completion
interactions.learnerAction: Disable author styles and complete the festival information and registration navigation tasks using headings, lists, links, form controls, and visible instructions.
interactions.artifactChange: Unstyled task baseline and failures are recorded.

evidence.observable: Core information and task sequence remain understandable and keyboard-operable without author CSS.
evidence.changedCase: One performance is canceled and registration instructions gain an additional prerequisite.

interactions.feedbackTarget: An unstyled page may lose meaning because CSS will restore it for every user.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair missing content or semantic relationships in HTML before any visual rule.

interactions.title: Predict festival keyboard sequence
interactions.learnerAction: Write the expected keyboard path through skip link, navigation, schedule links, registration fields, and submit control before any author presentation changes.
interactions.artifactChange: Keyboard baseline becomes retained-skill evidence.

evidence.observable: Predicted path follows meaningful source order and includes every interactive task without positive tabindex.
evidence.changedCase: A venue link and optional registration choice are inserted into the document.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Future CSS can safely establish keyboard order from visual priority.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace focusable elements in serialized DOM order and compare them with task dependencies.

interactions.title: Choose diagnostics before editing
interactions.learnerAction: Select validation, source, DOM, network, matched-rule, computed-style, keyboard, or reload evidence for each seeded festival defect hypothesis.
interactions.artifactChange: Diagnostic decision table is attached to the artifact.

evidence.observable: Every hypothesis has a surface that can disprove it and a first expected observation.
evidence.changedCase: A missing heading, wrong link path, invalid declaration, and unsaved source produce similar visual symptoms.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Opening every developer panel or repeatedly refreshing is an evidence plan.
interactions.correctionRoute: State one causal hypothesis and name the earliest consumer that would expose it.

interactions.title: Defend unchanged semantic markup
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why festival headings, navigation, schedule lists, labels, and buttons remain semantic HTML while classes are added only as proportional presentation hooks.
interactions.artifactChange: HTML preservation rationale is checkpointed.

evidence.observable: Explanation preserves native meanings and introduces hooks without replacing structure with generic containers.
evidence.changedCase: Festival branding changes while identical navigation and registration tasks remain.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Presentation work begins by converting semantic elements to generic elements with classes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare user and accessibility tasks before and after the proposed markup replacement.

interactions.title: Predict first declaration evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the match, computed value, visible consequence, unaffected content, and reload result for the festival body's first author declaration.
interactions.artifactChange: First declaration prediction is recorded before code.

evidence.observable: Prediction names source, targeted element set, computed property, visible change, non-targets, and saved-state check.
evidence.changedCase: A second body section is inserted before the rule is authored.

interactions.feedbackTarget: The declaration is proven when any part of the preview changes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name exact selector, property, target set, computed value, and reload observation.

interactions.title: Author and save the first rule
interactions.learnerAction: Author one bounded body rule in the external festival stylesheet, save it, and avoid changing semantic HTML or unrelated properties.
interactions.artifactChange: First valid saved CSS rule enters the cumulative stylesheet.

evidence.observable: Saved source contains one valid intentionally targeted rule and the editor checkpoint records its exact change.
evidence.changedCase: The requested property value changes before the saved checkpoint.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Typing source into an unsaved buffer is equivalent to changing the durable artifact.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect current file diff, save state, and only the intended rule boundary.

interactions.title: Trace matched and computed evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the body element's matched rule, source location, declared value, computed result, and rendered consequence after the first rule loads.
interactions.artifactChange: Matched-rule and computed trace is saved beside source.

evidence.observable: Inspected rule points to the authored file and produces the expected computed property on every intended body instance.
evidence.changedCase: A browser default differs while the authored computed value remains explicit.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Preview similarity means the declaration was ignored or unnecessary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect author rule and computed property independently of visual salience.

interactions.title: Prove the saved checkpoint survives reload
interactions.learnerAction: Reload from saved files, re-run the first declaration check, and compare source hash, matched rule, computed value, and preview with the pre-reload checkpoint.
interactions.artifactChange: Reload-stable first-rule checkpoint is accepted.

evidence.observable: Saved source and browser evidence reproduce the intended match and computed result after reload.
evidence.changedCase: An unsaved experimental edit is made and discarded before the verified reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Autosaved preview state guarantees durable source without explicit evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Return to last saved file, reload, and compare exact rule and computed value.

interactions.title: Verify the page without author CSS
interactions.learnerAction: Disable the festival stylesheet, repeat core information and registration tasks, then restore it without changing content, names, order, or control behavior.
interactions.artifactChange: Styled and unstyled task comparison is recorded.

evidence.observable: Author CSS changes presentation while core meaning, controls, and logical task sequence remain available.
evidence.changedCase: Browser author-style disabling occurs after an additional registration error message appears.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A stylesheet may hide required information because the normal visual state contains it.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair required content and relationships in HTML, then reapply presentation.

interactions.title: Explain the first source-preview loop
interactions.learnerAction: Explain the exact sequence from saved stylesheet source through parsing, selector match, declared value, computed value, rendering, checkpoint, and reload verification.
interactions.artifactChange: Causal first-rule explanation becomes mastery evidence.

evidence.observable: Explanation distinguishes every evidence layer and states what each observation can and cannot prove.
evidence.changedCase: The preview remains visually similar despite a different valid computed value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS editing is a direct source-to-pixel pipeline with no matching, computation, or persistence checks.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reconstruct the chain using one observation per browser or file layer.

interactions.title: Label festival rule anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Annotate the festival rule's selector, block, declaration, property, value, punctuation, whitespace, and complete qualified-rule boundary in the editor.
interactions.artifactChange: Rule anatomy overlay is stored with first stylesheet.

evidence.observable: Every token and nested unit receives its correct syntax role without calling one declaration a rule.
evidence.changedCase: A second declaration with a multi-token value is appended.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selector, property, declaration, and rule are interchangeable names.
interactions.correctionRoute: Start at property-value pair and expand through declaration, block, prelude, and rule.

interactions.title: Add a second declaration
interactions.learnerAction: Add one stakeholder-required declaration inside the existing festival rule using correct colon, value, terminator, and block placement.
interactions.artifactChange: Existing rule gains one valid bounded declaration.

evidence.observable: New declaration parses within intended block and contributes its own inspected computed value.
evidence.changedCase: The new value changes from one token to a two-token value.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A property-value line outside braces will apply to every prior selector.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate intended declaration block and verify delimiter roles before saving.

interactions.title: Predict an invalid property name
interactions.learnerAction: Predict parser, matched-rule, computed-style, and sibling-declaration results when the new festival property name is misspelled.
interactions.artifactChange: Unknown-property recovery prediction is committed.

evidence.observable: Prediction discards the unsupported declaration while preserving separately valid declarations in the same rule.
evidence.changedCase: The typo moves from property name to a required value keyword.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One unknown property invalidates all declarations in its rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect declaration-level support and then each sibling computed result.

interactions.title: Test declaration-level recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Introduce the bounded property typo, inspect which declaration disappears from computed evidence, verify siblings survive, then repair and retest.
interactions.artifactChange: Declaration-recovery test and repair are saved.

evidence.observable: Test isolates one rejected declaration, surviving siblings, exact repair, and restored computed result.
evidence.changedCase: A later valid declaration is reordered before the malformed declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Parser recovery can be inferred from unchanged appearance without inspecting siblings.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare parsed declarations and computed properties before and after one-token repair.

interactions.title: Predict an invalid selector
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether the entire festival style rule or only one declaration survives when its selector prelude becomes invalid.
interactions.artifactChange: Invalid-selector recovery boundary is recorded.

evidence.observable: Prediction rejects the affected qualified rule and does not misattribute the failure to valid declaration syntax.
evidence.changedCase: The invalid token moves from a class selector into a separate later rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Browsers ignore only the malformed selector token and keep applying its block globally.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate selector parsing from declaration-list parsing and inspect rule presence.

interactions.title: Inspect invalid-selector rule loss
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect source, parsed rule list, matched rules, and computed results after an invalid festival selector removes its rule from application.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector parse failure trace is saved.

evidence.observable: Evidence shows valid declarations are unavailable because their containing rule failed at selector parsing.
evidence.changedCase: A neighboring valid rule targets the same element with a different property.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Unmatched and invalid selectors are identical because neither changes pixels.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check whether rule parsed before checking whether its selector matched.

interactions.title: Add a maintenance comment
interactions.learnerAction: Insert a concise CSS comment that records the festival style decision without swallowing later rules or duplicating visible content.
interactions.artifactChange: Bounded maintenance comment is added to stylesheet.

evidence.observable: Comment delimiters close before later rules and commentary explains a presentation decision rather than supplying user content.
evidence.changedCase: A second rule is inserted immediately after the comment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS comments are safe places for information users need to complete tasks.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate maintenance rationale from document content and inspect comment termination.

interactions.title: Test comment termination
interactions.learnerAction: Temporarily remove the festival comment terminator, predict affected source range, inspect parsed-rule loss, restore the delimiter, and verify later rules return.
interactions.artifactChange: Comment-boundary failure and recovery evidence is stored.

evidence.observable: Test identifies the exact later source region consumed as comment and confirms repaired rules parse and match again.
evidence.changedCase: The comment moves above two rules instead of one.

interactions.feedbackTarget: An unfinished comment can affect only its own line.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect tokenization from opening delimiter through end of file or closing delimiter.

interactions.title: Distinguish at-rule from style rule
interactions.learnerAction: Label a bounded provided at-rule example and contrast its name, prelude, block or terminator with the festival selector-led qualified rules.
interactions.artifactChange: At-rule and style-rule distinction is added to anatomy evidence.

evidence.observable: Learner identifies syntax category without pretending every at-rule contains declarations or selects elements.
evidence.changedCase: Provided at-rule changes from statement form to block form.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every brace-delimited CSS construct is a selector followed by declarations.
interactions.correctionRoute: Check whether construct begins with at-keyword or qualified-rule prelude.

interactions.title: Explain bounded CSS recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why malformed selector, property name, property value, block delimiter, and comment boundary failures have different recovery scopes in the festival stylesheet.
interactions.artifactChange: Parser recovery explanation closes rule-anatomy checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Each failure is connected to its current parse unit, observable loss, surviving source, and minimal repair.
evidence.changedCase: The same visual symptom is produced once by a bad value and once by a bad selector.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS either fails as a whole or guesses the author's intended repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name parsing context, discarded unit, next recoverable boundary, and surviving evidence.

interactions.title: Choose festival CSS application method
interactions.learnerAction: Choose external stylesheet, head style block, or inline declaration for a changed three-page festival requirement and predict maintenance and reuse consequences.
interactions.artifactChange: Application-method decision and rejected alternatives are committed.

evidence.observable: Choice follows reuse, ownership, cache, order, and maintenance constraints rather than convenience alone.
evidence.changedCase: Festival expands from one page to three pages sharing the same presentation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Inline declarations are most maintainable because they cannot fail to load separately.
interactions.correctionRoute: Count owners and reuse sites, then trace update and delivery consequences.

interactions.title: Link the external festival stylesheet
interactions.learnerAction: Add the stylesheet relationship to document metadata with the exact relative reference needed by the current festival project tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete document now declares one external stylesheet relationship.

evidence.observable: Relationship is in the head, resolves from document URL, and identifies the intended CSS resource.
evidence.changedCase: Stylesheet moves from the document directory into an assets/styles directory.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Relative resource references resolve from repository root regardless of document URL.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve reference against document URL and compare with actual project tree.

interactions.title: Inspect the stylesheet request
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect the festival stylesheet request URL, status, redirects, response headers, media type, transferred body, initiator, and timing before checking styles.
interactions.artifactChange: Network evidence is linked to stylesheet checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Request reaches exact resource and returns usable CSS evidence without an HTML error document masquerading as success.
evidence.changedCase: Server changes missing-resource behavior from 404 to an HTML response with status 200.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Status 200 alone proves a valid stylesheet response.
interactions.correctionRoute: Verify final URL, media type, body content, and parsed rule availability together.

interactions.title: Test a changed relative path
interactions.learnerAction: Move a parallel festival document one directory deeper, predict its stylesheet URL, run the load check, and repair only the broken relationship.
interactions.artifactChange: Nested-document load case passes with exact repaired path.

evidence.observable: Both documents resolve intended stylesheet from their own URLs and expose same parsed rule evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Second document moves from sibling directory to a nested venue directory.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A path that works from one document automatically works from every document.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve each relative reference from each document URL before editing files.

interactions.title: Compare internal and inline ownership
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect bounded internal and inline examples, identify their document ownership and update surfaces, then compare them with the shared festival stylesheet.
interactions.artifactChange: Application ownership comparison is saved.

evidence.observable: Comparison identifies actual reuse, source location, update count, and inspection path for each method.
evidence.changedCase: A formerly unique presentation requirement becomes shared by all festival pages.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Application methods differ only in syntax and have identical maintenance boundaries.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace each declaration to owner, reuse sites, update sites, and browser evidence.

interactions.title: Test wrong response media type
interactions.learnerAction: Run a fixture where the festival CSS URL returns HTML, compare network and rule evidence, then restore the CSS response and verify parsing resumes.
interactions.artifactChange: Wrong-response diagnosis and verified recovery are recorded.

evidence.observable: Test separates successful transport from usable stylesheet content and confirms parsed rules after repair.
evidence.changedCase: Error response changes between an HTML login page and an HTML not-found page.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A response body loaded at a CSS URL is always interpreted as stylesheet source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect response media type, body, parsed stylesheet, matched rules, and computation.

interactions.title: Test stylesheet source order
interactions.learnerAction: Reorder two bounded festival stylesheets with an intentional same-property conflict, inspect resulting matched rules, then restore documented ownership order.
interactions.artifactChange: Load-order consequence and restoration are checkpointed.

evidence.observable: Evidence connects document relationship order to parsed source order and changed computed value without teaching full cascade.
evidence.changedCase: The conflict moves from a type selector to identical class selectors.

interactions.feedbackTarget: If both files load successfully, their document order cannot affect results.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect relationship order, rule order, matching declarations, and computed property.

interactions.title: Defend external stylesheet choice
interactions.learnerAction: Defend the shared external festival stylesheet using page ownership, reuse, cache opportunity, update count, load evidence, and bounded failure handling.
interactions.artifactChange: Application-method defense closes loading checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Defense names benefits, costs, verification, and changed constraints that would justify another method.
evidence.changedCase: Festival becomes a single offline document with one truly unique declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: External CSS is always correct regardless of ownership, delivery, or reuse.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tie method to current constraint evidence and state its invalidation condition.

interactions.title: Predict the type-selector match set
interactions.learnerAction: Predict every current and newly inserted festival paragraph matched by a type selector before adding the rule.
interactions.artifactChange: Paragraph match-set prediction is committed.

evidence.observable: Prediction includes all elements of the named type and excludes headings, list items, and generic containers.
evidence.changedCase: A paragraph is added inside the registration section and another is removed from the introduction.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Type selectors match only elements near the rule's authoring context.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate the document tree for exact element type independent of location.

interactions.title: Author a proportional type rule
interactions.learnerAction: Add a festival paragraph type rule only after confirming the presentation requirement truly applies to every paragraph.
interactions.artifactChange: One justified type-selector rule enters the stylesheet.

evidence.observable: Rule parses and every current paragraph receives the intended computed property without extra hooks.
evidence.changedCase: A legal-disclosure paragraph is added and must receive the same treatment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Type rules are safe even when only one current element needs the style.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restate required match set and switch to reusable class if type-wide claim is false.

interactions.title: Predict a reusable class set
interactions.learnerAction: Select three festival elements with the same presentation role across different semantic types and predict the match set for one reusable class.
interactions.artifactChange: Cross-type reusable class plan is recorded.

evidence.observable: Class represents a reusable presentation role and matches exact intended elements without changing their semantics.
evidence.changedCase: A fourth element of a new semantic type gains the same presentation role.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Classes can repeat only on elements of the same HTML type.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate semantic element type from reusable presentation grouping.

interactions.title: Add the reusable festival class
interactions.learnerAction: Add one reusable presentation class to exact intended festival elements and author its rule without replacing element semantics or duplicating identifiers.
interactions.artifactChange: Reusable class hook and matching rule are saved.

evidence.observable: All and only intended elements match class rule while retaining native roles, names, and source order.
evidence.changedCase: One intended element changes from paragraph to list item without losing presentation role.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Changing an element to a generic container is required for class-based styling.
interactions.correctionRoute: Keep semantic element and add only the proportional class token.

interactions.title: Inspect class matches after insertion
interactions.learnerAction: Insert a new festival item with and without the reusable class, then inspect exact matched rules and computed differences for both cases.
interactions.artifactChange: Changed class-membership evidence is saved.

evidence.observable: Membership token determines intended class match while other type rules remain independently observable.
evidence.changedCase: New item initially omits class, then receives it without any element-type change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A class selector matches elements whose text or visual role resembles its name.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect literal class-token membership and matched-rule list.

interactions.title: Predict a unique identifier match
interactions.learnerAction: Predict the single current element matched by the festival venue identifier and state why duplicated identifiers would break identity evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Unique-identifier match prediction is committed.

evidence.observable: Identifier selector matches exactly one semantically justified unique target and duplicate state is rejected.
evidence.changedCase: A second venue card is copied with the same identifier before validation.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Identifiers are ideal reusable styling hooks because selectors are concise.
interactions.correctionRoute: Distinguish unique target identity from repeated presentation membership.

interactions.title: Test duplicate identifier failure
interactions.learnerAction: Duplicate the venue identifier in a fixture, inspect selector matches and validation evidence, then replace repeated styling intent with a class.
interactions.artifactChange: Duplicate-identifier correction and class migration are recorded.

evidence.observable: Test exposes invalid duplicate identity and repair preserves repeated styling through a reusable class.
evidence.changedCase: Venue list grows from one unique featured location to three equally styled locations.

interactions.feedbackTarget: If an ID selector styles every copied element, duplicate IDs are harmless.
interactions.correctionRoute: Reserve identifier for unique identity and encode repeated presentation membership as class.

interactions.title: Predict universal selector reach
interactions.learnerAction: Predict every festival element reached by a universal selector and identify browser-default or form-control effects that broad styling could unintentionally erase.
interactions.artifactChange: Universal reach and risk map is committed.

evidence.observable: Prediction includes all element nodes in scope and names specific retained semantics or usability not proven by broad visual normalization.
evidence.changedCase: A new native form control and nested metadata element are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Universal selector affects only visible text containers and has no coupling cost.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate all elements including controls and newly inserted descendants before authoring broad rules.

interactions.title: Reject accidental universal reset
interactions.learnerAction: Apply a risky universal fixture, inspect changed control and focus presentation, remove it, and implement narrower type or class hooks that satisfy the festival need.
interactions.artifactChange: Broad-reset rejection and narrow-selector repair are saved.

evidence.observable: Repair matches only intended elements and retains usable native control and focus evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Browser introduces a newly rendered native control state not present in initial fixture.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A universal reset is proven safe when current screenshot looks cleaner.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare exact match set and retained control states, then narrow selector to actual requirement.

interactions.title: Defend final selector set
interactions.learnerAction: Defend each festival universal, type, class, or identifier decision using intended match set, reuse, uniqueness, semantic stability, future insertion, and inspected evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector defense closes guided stylesheet checkpoint.

evidence.observable: Every selector has a bounded current and changed match set plus a stated reason rejected hooks were more coupled.
evidence.changedCase: Festival gains a second page and repeats one presentation role across new element types.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Shortest selector or strongest identifier is automatically most maintainable.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare intended membership, semantic ownership, reuse, uniqueness, and changed markup before defending syntax.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A separate library-exhibit page receives a maintainable first stylesheet from partial evidence prompts, with fewer hints and no reuse of festival source, requirement order, or visual choices.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives original semantic exhibit HTML, a partially connected empty stylesheet, changed user tasks, and incomplete evidence slots rather than the guided festival artifact.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner completes and explains fifteen increasingly independent decisions covering retained HTML, source persistence, rule parsing, loading, and proportional selector matching.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback states the observed failed invariant and names one evidence surface; it does not identify the final declaration, selector, path, or complete repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: Faded hints provide a concept cue first, a source or evidence region only after another attempt, and bounded syntax only after a recorded explanation.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A miss opens a smaller exhibit fragment with a different changed case, then requires the learner to transfer the correction back without copied source.

interactions.title: Complete exhibit layer classification
interactions.learnerAction: Complete missing layer assignments for exhibit title meaning, featured appearance, filter behavior, and enlarged user text while preserving semantic HTML responsibility.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit responsibility map becomes complete.

evidence.observable: Assignments preserve content and meaning in HTML, presentation in CSS, behavior in script, and preferences under user control.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative label becomes required collection context.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Anything seen on screen can be moved into CSS without information loss.
interactions.correctionRoute: Disable author styles and inspect which requirement must remain perceivable.

interactions.title: Test exhibit document and order
interactions.learnerAction: Validate the complete exhibit document and run its unstyled reading and keyboard path after a new collection action is inserted.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete-document and task-order baseline passes.

evidence.observable: Document envelope remains complete and source, reading, and keyboard order still follow exhibit task dependencies.
evidence.changedCase: A reserve-item control is inserted between description and related resources.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS can later correct any awkward source or keyboard order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair serialized structure and task order before styling.

interactions.title: Choose exhibit diagnostic surfaces
interactions.learnerAction: Explain which evidence distinguishes an incomplete document, missing stylesheet, invalid selector, unmatched class, ignored declaration, and unsaved edit in the exhibit workspace.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit diagnostic routing explanation is saved.

evidence.observable: Each failure maps to a distinct source, validator, network, rule, computed, or persistence observation.
evidence.changedCase: Two failures are combined while preview remains partially styled.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Opening computed styles is enough to distinguish every source and load defect.
interactions.correctionRoute: Locate earliest layer where each hypothesis predicts different evidence.

interactions.title: Preserve exhibit semantic hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Add presentation hooks to the exhibit without replacing its heading, article, list, link, or button semantics and without changing logical source order.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit markup gains bounded hooks with semantics intact.

evidence.observable: Hook additions leave native elements, accessible tasks, and source order unchanged.
evidence.changedCase: One exhibit action changes from link navigation to button behavior before hook review.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Generic containers provide cleaner CSS even when native meaning is lost.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore task-appropriate element, then add only needed class or unique identifier.

interactions.title: Complete exhibit evidence chain
interactions.learnerAction: Fill missing source, match, computed, rendered, saved, and reloaded observations for the exhibit's first declaration.
interactions.artifactChange: Incomplete evidence chain becomes causally complete.

evidence.observable: Every stage has one direct observation and no screenshot substitutes for match or persistence.
evidence.changedCase: Declared value changes while rendered difference becomes visually subtle.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Source and screenshot are the only evidence layers worth keeping.
interactions.correctionRoute: Insert missing browser computation and durable-file observations.

interactions.title: Write an exhibit presentation rule
interactions.learnerAction: Author and save one exhibit presentation rule from the stakeholder request while leaving required collection meaning in HTML.
interactions.artifactChange: First exhibit rule is authored and checkpointed.

evidence.observable: Rule source is saved, matches intended elements, yields expected computed value, and survives reload.
evidence.changedCase: Featured-exhibit status moves to a different article.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Required featured status may exist only as a color supplied by CSS.
interactions.correctionRoute: Preserve status text or semantics in HTML, then style its presentation hook.

interactions.title: Test exhibit persistence and no-style use
interactions.learnerAction: Reload the saved exhibit, inspect the computed declaration, disable author styles, and complete the collection task in both states.
interactions.artifactChange: Persistence and unstyled-use evidence are accepted.

evidence.observable: Saved styling reproduces after reload while unstyled task remains meaningful and keyboard-operable.
evidence.changedCase: Collection description grows and adds a second action.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Persistence proof and no-style task proof are the same observation.
interactions.correctionRoute: Run reload evidence and author-style-disabled task evidence separately.

interactions.title: Complete malformed rule diagnosis
interactions.learnerAction: Complete the parser diagnosis for one exhibit selector error and one declaration-value error, naming rejected unit and surviving rules.
interactions.artifactChange: Two parser recovery records become complete.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis distinguishes rule-level selector loss from declaration-level value loss and proves surviving source.
evidence.changedCase: Errors trade positions between first and last stylesheet rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Same missing visual effect means same parser recovery boundary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify grammar context before comparing resulting parsed units.

interactions.title: Repair exhibit declaration block
interactions.learnerAction: Repair missing delimiter and misplaced declaration in the exhibit rule using the smallest syntax change, then preserve all valid sibling declarations.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit rule parses with siblings unchanged.

evidence.observable: Minimal repair restores intended declaration and leaves independent computed sibling values stable.
evidence.changedCase: Sibling order changes before the same malformed declaration is reintroduced.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Replacing the entire rule is safest because parser failures cannot be localized.
interactions.correctionRoute: Find first divergent token and change only its grammar boundary.

interactions.title: Test exhibit stylesheet relocation
interactions.learnerAction: Relocate the exhibit stylesheet, predict resolved request, update the relationship, and verify URL, media type, parsed rule, and computation.
interactions.artifactChange: Relocated stylesheet passes full load trace.

evidence.observable: Updated document relationship reaches exact CSS response and restores intended rule contribution.
evidence.changedCase: Stylesheet filename and parent directory both change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Updating project tree is enough; browser will discover moved CSS automatically.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve document reference and follow request through parsed rule evidence.

interactions.title: Explain exhibit application choice
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why shared exhibit presentation remains external while one provided internal and one inline alternative fail current ownership and maintenance constraints.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit application-method rationale is saved.

evidence.observable: Explanation uses current reuse, update, ownership, and delivery evidence and states when decision would change.
evidence.changedCase: Exhibit collapses to a single printable handout with one unique override.

interactions.feedbackTarget: External CSS requires no justification because it is always preferred.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare current owners, consumers, update count, and delivery boundary.

interactions.title: Complete exhibit selector predictions
interactions.learnerAction: Complete exact match sets for provided exhibit universal, type, class, and identifier selectors after two new items are inserted.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector prediction table covers changed markup.

evidence.observable: Each selector lists exact matching elements, intended membership, and any accidental reach.
evidence.changedCase: A new article repeats a class and accidentally duplicates an identifier.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selector names communicate intent, so tree enumeration is unnecessary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate literal selector against every changed element and token.

interactions.title: Replace overcoupled exhibit hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Replace repeated identifier styling and one overbroad type rule with proportional reusable classes while keeping unique fragment identity intact.
interactions.artifactChange: Exhibit hooks now match reuse and identity boundaries.

evidence.observable: Repeated presentation uses classes, unique navigation identity remains unique, and unrelated types stop matching.
evidence.changedCase: One styled item changes semantic element type and one unique target remains addressable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Identifier selectors should be duplicated whenever the declaration is reused.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate unique identity from repeated presentation membership before editing selectors.

interactions.title: Test exhibit selector growth
interactions.learnerAction: Add a new exhibit type and presentation role, then prove existing selectors neither miss intended elements nor capture unrelated content or controls.
interactions.artifactChange: Selector growth regression evidence passes.

evidence.observable: Changed markup produces intended match sets, stable computed values, and unchanged control and keyboard behavior.
evidence.changedCase: A new aside contains both styled metadata and an unstylized native button.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Current screenshot proves selectors will remain proportional after insertion.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate new elements, inspect matches, and narrow or extend only from explicit membership.

interactions.title: Explain exhibit first-stylesheet evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Explain how complete semantic source, logical order, targeted inspection, saved rules, verified loading, parser recovery, and proportional selectors jointly protect the exhibit.
interactions.artifactChange: Faded exhibit defense becomes completion evidence.

evidence.observable: Defense connects every concept to observed current and changed-case evidence without claiming authored content or publication.
evidence.changedCase: Exhibit adds a second page, a moved stylesheet, and a repeated presentation role.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A visually attractive exhibit is sufficient evidence for all source and browser layers.
interactions.correctionRoute: Attach one bounded observation to each document, CSS, loading, selector, computation, and persistence claim.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A layered clinic record diagnoses and repairs ten visually similar failures without guessing, whole-file replacement, semantic regression, or confusing invalid, unloaded, unmatched, overridden, unsupported, and unsaved states.
activityDesigns.startingState: Ten unrelated client defects share a mostly unchanged preview but differ across HTML structure, source order, stylesheet transport, parser, selector matching, computation, and persistence.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner predicts, inspects, debugs, tests, and explains the earliest divergent evidence and smallest repair for every defect while preserving prior invariants.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback reports the first observed contradiction between hypothesis and evidence, never the final diagnosis or replacement source.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On request, hints narrow from evidence layer to inspection surface to one suspicious boundary; they never reveal canonical repair.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Wrong diagnosis must be falsified against a changed parallel trace before learner revises hypothesis and returns to the original clinic case.

interactions.title: Predict structure versus presentation defect
interactions.learnerAction: Predict whether missing event context belongs to HTML meaning or CSS presentation before inspecting the client's apparently blank badge.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer hypothesis is committed before evidence opens.

evidence.observable: Prediction distinguishes absent required content from present content made visually inconspicuous.
evidence.changedCase: Author styles are disabled and badge wording changes.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every invisible item is a CSS defect even when required content is absent.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect source and unstyled task before changing presentation.

interactions.title: Inspect broken document and keyboard path
interactions.learnerAction: Inspect complete source, parsed DOM, and keyboard sequence to determine why a client action is outside the expected body task flow.
interactions.artifactChange: Structural root-cause trace is saved.

evidence.observable: Evidence locates malformed document ownership and proves CSS is not the first repair layer.
evidence.changedCase: The action moves into valid body source but remains in wrong task order.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A display property is the safest repair for any misplaced action.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair complete document and source order, then reassess presentation.

interactions.title: Debug an unsaved declaration
interactions.learnerAction: Distinguish editor buffer, saved file, served source, matched rule, and computed result to repair a client change that vanished on reload.
interactions.artifactChange: Unsaved-state cause and durable repair are recorded.

evidence.observable: Trace proves preview used transient source and reload used older saved source before repair.
evidence.changedCase: A second unsaved edit affects another property before reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Reload loss means browser rejected otherwise saved CSS.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare buffer, file, response, match, computation, and reload in order.

interactions.title: Test intentional no-style resilience
interactions.learnerAction: Disable client author CSS, run the complete task and keyboard path, restore styles, and repair any meaning that presentation had improperly supplied.
interactions.artifactChange: No-style task regression passes after semantic repair.

evidence.observable: Required meaning and sequence survive while presentation remains separately restorable.
evidence.changedCase: A status previously expressed only by color receives changed wording.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Normal styled use is enough; author CSS absence need not preserve task meaning.
interactions.correctionRoute: Move required information into HTML and leave its visual treatment in CSS.

interactions.title: Debug invalid selector versus value
interactions.learnerAction: Use parsed-rule and computed evidence to distinguish one invalid selector from one invalid property value in adjacent client rules.
interactions.artifactChange: Two parser failures receive separate minimal repairs.

evidence.observable: Rule-level and declaration-level loss are separately observed and corrected without replacing valid siblings.
evidence.changedCase: The invalid value moves into a rule whose selector matches multiple elements.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Identical preview symptoms justify identical whole-rule replacement.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect rule presence, declaration presence, match, and computation before editing.

interactions.title: Debug swallowed client rules
interactions.learnerAction: Locate an unfinished comment that consumes later client rules, repair only its delimiter, and prove later parsed rules and matches return.
interactions.artifactChange: Comment boundary repair restores downstream rules.

evidence.observable: Source range, lost parsed rules, delimiter repair, and restored computed evidence are all recorded.
evidence.changedCase: A new valid rule is inserted inside the accidentally consumed region.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Later rule failures must originate inside each later rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Trace tokenizer state from last successfully parsed boundary.

interactions.title: Debug successful wrong resource
interactions.learnerAction: Trace a status-200 client stylesheet request that returns an HTML sign-in page and repair exact resource delivery before changing selectors.
interactions.artifactChange: Wrong-resource load cause and delivery repair are saved.

evidence.observable: Final URL, media type, response body, parsed rule absence, corrected CSS response, and restored match are proven.
evidence.changedCase: Redirect destination changes while transport status remains successful.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Successful status proves CSS content, so selector guessing is next.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect final response identity and parse result before rule matching.

interactions.title: Test moved client stylesheet
interactions.learnerAction: Change client document depth, predict broken relative URL, repair relationship, and test exact response and matched rule on both pages.
interactions.artifactChange: Both client routes now pass load evidence.

evidence.observable: Each document independently resolves and applies intended CSS resource after path change.
evidence.changedCase: One route gains an additional nested path segment.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A relationship repaired for one page proves every page's relative URL.
interactions.correctionRoute: Resolve and inspect each document-resource pair independently.

interactions.title: Debug unmatched class and duplicate ID
interactions.learnerAction: Distinguish a missing class token from duplicated identifier styling, then repair reusable membership and preserve one unique target.
interactions.artifactChange: Class membership and unique identity are repaired separately.

evidence.observable: Intended repeated items match class rule, one target retains unique ID, and validation no longer reports duplicate identity.
evidence.changedCase: A third repeated item is added without a class and with copied identifier.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Changing selector to universal fixes both missing class and duplicate identity safely.
interactions.correctionRoute: Enumerate intended repeated members and independent unique target before editing hooks.

interactions.title: Explain first-divergence diagnosis
interactions.learnerAction: Explain why each clinic case first diverged at semantic source, document order, saved state, parser, transport, selector match, or computation and why the repair was minimal.
interactions.artifactChange: Clinic diagnosis defense becomes completion evidence.

evidence.observable: Each conclusion cites falsifying evidence, first divergence, retained invariants, minimal repair, and changed-case retest.
evidence.changedCase: Two independent defects are combined in one new client trace.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual symptom categories determine root cause without layered evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Order evidence layers and locate earliest observation inconsistent with intended pipeline.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: An independently built neighborhood-event visual baseline starts from semantic HTML and empty CSS, then proves document integrity, source persistence, rule syntax, stylesheet delivery, exact selector sets, changed cases, and defensible scope.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives a stakeholder brief, authentic event content, an empty CSS file, independent acceptance evidence, and no festival or exhibit source, recipe, visual design, or requirement ordering.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner builds and defends a distinct first stylesheet through fifteen acceptance outcomes while preserving every retained HTML and keyboard invariant.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies only the failed user task, invariant, and observed evidence; learner chooses diagnosis and repair.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request lab hints point to a competency and evidence category, then a relevant artifact region after another attempt, never final selectors or declarations.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed requirement creates a smaller changed neighborhood case and parallel acceptance check before the learner retries the original artifact.

interactions.title: Build complete event document baseline
interactions.learnerAction: Build the neighborhood event as a complete semantic HTML document with truthful content and no dependence on author styling for meaning.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent complete event HTML baseline is saved.

evidence.observable: Document has complete envelope, meaningful structure, current content, and usable core tasks before CSS.
evidence.changedCase: Event language, title, and one schedule section change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: An empty body wrapper and future CSS are enough to start the lab.
interactions.correctionRoute: Complete document and task meaning before presentation work.

interactions.title: Test event source and keyboard order
interactions.learnerAction: Validate source, linearize event content, and traverse every link and control by keyboard before and after a new action is inserted.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent retained-skill baseline passes.

evidence.observable: Source is conforming within scope and logical reading and keyboard paths follow complete event tasks.
evidence.changedCase: An accessibility-request action is inserted before registration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual order planned for later CSS can substitute for source-order evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair serialized relationships and focus sequence before any selector work.

interactions.title: Create event stylesheet checkpoint
interactions.learnerAction: Create, link, author, and save the event's first bounded presentation rule without importing prior activity source or moving semantic content into CSS.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent first stylesheet checkpoint is created.

evidence.observable: Original saved rule loads, matches intended elements, computes expected value, and leaves semantic task intact.
evidence.changedCase: Event stakeholder changes one presentation value after initial save.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Copying the workshop rule and renaming its class proves independent construction.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive rule from current event requirement and empty source.

interactions.title: Test event source-to-reload chain
interactions.learnerAction: Prove the event declaration through saved source, parsed rule, exact match, computed result, visible output, reload, and last passing checkpoint.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent persistence trace passes.

evidence.observable: Every pipeline stage has direct evidence and reproduces from durable files.
evidence.changedCase: A transient unsaved edit is introduced between passing checkpoint and reload.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Passing preview before reload proves durable event source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare durable file, served CSS, match, computation, and reloaded output.

interactions.title: Test event without author styling
interactions.learnerAction: Disable the independent event stylesheet and complete information, navigation, and registration tasks without loss of required meaning or logical order.
interactions.artifactChange: No-author-style acceptance case passes.

evidence.observable: Every required event task remains understandable and operable when author presentation is unavailable.
evidence.changedCase: A visual featured state becomes a changed event status.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Required state may exist solely through event color because styled mode is primary.
interactions.correctionRoute: Encode state meaning in HTML, then style its presentation independently.

interactions.title: Build valid event rule set
interactions.learnerAction: Author a small original event rule set with correctly bounded selectors, blocks, declarations, properties, values, terminators, and maintenance comments.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent event rule set is saved.

evidence.observable: Every rule parses, each declaration has intentional ownership, and comments terminate before executable source.
evidence.changedCase: A new declaration with a multi-token value is added to one rule.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Valid appearance proves rule boundaries even if parser discarded unrelated source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect parsed rules and each intended declaration against authored source.

interactions.title: Test event parser mutations
interactions.learnerAction: Run invalid selector, invalid value, and unfinished comment mutations against the event rules and record distinct recovery boundaries.
interactions.artifactChange: Three independent parser mutation cases pass.

evidence.observable: Each mutation names rejected unit, surviving source, first visible consequence, and exact minimal repair.
evidence.changedCase: Mutations move to different positions and affect different target counts.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One generic invalid-CSS test covers every parser failure.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test one grammar boundary at a time and inspect parsed output.

interactions.title: Choose event application boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Choose external, internal, or inline application for the independent event based on actual page reuse, ownership, caching, and maintenance constraints.
interactions.artifactChange: Event application method and load relationship are implemented.

evidence.observable: Implemented method matches current constraints and exposes a verifiable source and delivery boundary.
evidence.changedCase: Event expands from one page to a schedule and venue page.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Method selection is a personal preference unrelated to reuse or ownership.
interactions.correctionRoute: Model changed update sites and delivery paths for each method.

interactions.title: Test event resource delivery
interactions.learnerAction: Verify event stylesheet URL, response identity, media type, source order, parsed rule, and computed contribution across every event page.
interactions.artifactChange: Multi-page load acceptance evidence passes.

evidence.observable: Each page reaches intended CSS resource and proves actual rule application rather than filename presence.
evidence.changedCase: Venue page moves into a nested directory.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One successful page proves shared resource loading everywhere.
interactions.correctionRoute: Inspect each document URL and relationship through computation.

interactions.title: Test event wrong-resource recovery
interactions.learnerAction: Replace one event CSS response with an HTML error fixture, diagnose transport versus parsing evidence, restore delivery, and verify no source workaround remains.
interactions.artifactChange: Wrong-resource failure and clean recovery are stored.

evidence.observable: Diagnosis identifies response identity before selector logic and recovery restores intended parsed rules without inline duplication.
evidence.changedCase: Wrong response returns successful status after a redirect.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Inline duplication is a valid repair for broken external delivery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Restore single owned resource path and remove compensating declarations.

interactions.title: Build event selector set
interactions.learnerAction: Build a proportional event selector set using type only for type-wide requirements, classes for reusable roles, and one identifier only for genuine unique identity.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent selector set and hooks are saved.

evidence.observable: Every selector's literal match set equals intended membership and no semantic element replacement or duplicate identity is introduced.
evidence.changedCase: A second event card and new semantic element reuse one presentation role.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every styled event element needs an ID to prevent class collisions.
interactions.correctionRoute: Assign hooks from reuse and identity rather than visual uniqueness.

interactions.title: Test event selector insertions
interactions.learnerAction: Insert new event content with repeated and non-repeated roles, then inspect exact selector matches and computed changes without editing selectors first.
interactions.artifactChange: Insertion match-set regression passes.

evidence.observable: New elements inherit only type-wide treatment and class treatment explicitly represented by membership.
evidence.changedCase: New content changes from paragraph to list item while retaining one reusable class.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selectors infer presentation roles from nearby content or text.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate literal type, class tokens, and unique IDs on changed tree.

interactions.title: Test event universal-selector risk
interactions.learnerAction: Evaluate a proposed universal event rule against every element, native control, and changed insertion, then reject or bound it from observed consequences.
interactions.artifactChange: Universal-selector decision includes full reach evidence.

evidence.observable: Decision accounts for all matched elements and preserves control, focus, task, and insertion behavior.
evidence.changedCase: A new native select and validation message enter the form.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Universal rules are harmless when declaration looks cosmetic.
interactions.correctionRoute: Test every matched control and state, then narrow to real requirement.

interactions.title: Run event duplicate-identity mutation
interactions.learnerAction: Copy an event component with its identifier, observe validation and selector consequences, then restore unique identity and reusable class membership.
interactions.artifactChange: Duplicate-identity mutation and correction pass.

evidence.observable: Copied component no longer duplicates identity, repeated presentation still applies, and unique target remains exact.
evidence.changedCase: Three component copies are inserted instead of one.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Repeated ID styling is acceptable if JavaScript is not yet present.
interactions.correctionRoute: Treat document identity as semantic relationship independent of current script use.

interactions.title: Defend neighborhood visual baseline
interactions.learnerAction: Defend independent event architecture, HTML/CSS boundary, source loop, rule syntax, load method, selector sets, changed tests, tradeoffs, and unresolved presentation scope.
interactions.artifactChange: Independent lab evidence package is submitted for review.

evidence.observable: Every decision cites current artifact evidence, changed-case behavior, rejected alternatives, and bounded conclusions.
evidence.changedCase: Stakeholder adds a new page, content type, repeated role, and user text preference.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual polish and source volume prove first-CSS mastery.
interactions.correctionRoute: Tie each claim to observable user, source, load, parser, selector, computation, or persistence evidence.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A delayed retrieval record reconstructs the first-CSS mental model from mixed new scenarios, corrects misconceptions, schedules parallel review, and links every answer to changed evidence rather than recognition.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner sees unfamiliar short source, network, parser, match, computed, no-style, keyboard, and persistence traces without access to prior workshop answers.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner retrieves all nine concepts, corrects each missed causal distinction, and completes a different parallel case before review evidence is accepted.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: Feedback identifies observed misconception and missing evidence category after commitment; it does not reveal canonical answer before correction attempt.
activityDesigns.hintContract: On-request review hints first cue retrieval category, then contrast two possible evidence layers, then provide a smaller parallel case.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: Every miss requires an explanation, smaller worked contrast, and a different changed-case answer before delayed review is rescheduled.

interactions.title: Retrieve HTML and CSS responsibilities
interactions.learnerAction: Retrieve whether changed content meaning, native task structure, visual emphasis, and dynamic disclosure belong to HTML, CSS, or script.
interactions.artifactChange: Layer-boundary retrieval answer is committed.

evidence.observable: Required meaning and native behavior remain in HTML while presentation request is assigned to CSS.
evidence.changedCase: A decorative mark becomes required warning text.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Any visible change is necessarily CSS.
interactions.correctionRoute: Ask what remains meaningful and operable with author styles absent.

interactions.title: Retrieve complete document placement
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct where doctype, root language, metadata, title, stylesheet relationship, and visible content belong in a complete changed document.
interactions.artifactChange: Complete-document placement answer is stored.

evidence.observable: Document envelope and CSS relationship ownership are reconstructed without hidden scaffold.
evidence.changedCase: Document gets a nested route and a new language.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A stylesheet link may be placed with visible body controls.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate metadata relationships from body task content.

interactions.title: Predict logical order under styling
interactions.learnerAction: Predict source, reading, and keyboard order after a presentation-only prominence change and identify any claim CSS cannot prove.
interactions.artifactChange: Order prediction and evidence limit are recorded.

evidence.observable: Logical task order stays grounded in source and keyboard evidence regardless of visual emphasis.
evidence.changedCase: Prominent action moves visually while source remains fixed.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual prominence defines keyboard priority.
interactions.correctionRoute: Traverse source and focus order independently from visual placement.

interactions.title: Retrieve diagnostic evidence choices
interactions.learnerAction: Select evidence surfaces for malformed document, missing response, invalid rule, unmatched selector, changed computation, and unsaved source.
interactions.artifactChange: Diagnostic retrieval table is completed.

evidence.observable: Each failure maps to a falsifying source, network, parser, match, computed, or persistence observation.
evidence.changedCase: Two failures share an unchanged preview.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Validator output diagnoses stylesheet transport and save state.
interactions.correctionRoute: Name actual consumer of each artifact layer.

interactions.title: Retrieve source-to-persistence loop
interactions.learnerAction: Reconstruct saved source, parsing, selector match, declaration, computed value, rendering, checkpoint, and reload sequence from shuffled evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Shuffled evidence is ordered causally.

evidence.observable: Sequence distinguishes each stage and identifies what failure each observation can falsify.
evidence.changedCase: Computed value stays same while saved declaration source changes.
