interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendering always changes whenever source changes.
interactions.correctionRoute: Order by browser and file dependencies, not visual salience.

interactions.title: Correct visual-proof misconception
interactions.learnerAction: Correct the claim that changed pixels prove intended selector, winning declaration, supported value, and saved source.
interactions.artifactChange: Misconception correction includes missing evidence.

evidence.observable: Correction demands matched rule, computed value, source identity, saved state, and reload evidence.
evidence.changedCase: A different existing rule produces same visual color.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A screenshot uniquely identifies source and persistence.
interactions.correctionRoute: List alternative causes compatible with pixels and inspect discriminating evidence.

interactions.title: Explain no-style boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Explain which information and task behavior must survive disabled author CSS and which browser or user presentation may legitimately differ.
interactions.artifactChange: No-style boundary explanation is saved.

evidence.observable: Explanation preserves required meaning and logical operation without demanding identical appearance.
evidence.changedCase: User increases text size and disables author colors.

interactions.feedbackTarget: No-style resilience means pixels must remain identical.
interactions.correctionRoute: Separate task invariants from author-specific visual choices.

interactions.title: Retrieve rule anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Label selector, rule, block, declaration, property, value, terminators, comment, and at-rule in unfamiliar mixed CSS source.
interactions.artifactChange: Mixed-source anatomy labels are committed.

evidence.observable: Every component receives correct parsing role without conflating at-rule and qualified rule.
evidence.changedCase: Source gains a block at-rule and another declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Every property-value pair is an independent rule.
interactions.correctionRoute: Expand from declaration tokens to their containing syntax unit.

interactions.title: Predict parser recovery scopes
interactions.learnerAction: Predict surviving source after invalid selector, unknown property, invalid value, missing brace, and unfinished comment mutations.
interactions.artifactChange: Recovery-scope predictions are stored.

evidence.observable: Predictions distinguish declaration, rule, block, and remaining-source consequences.
evidence.changedCase: Each malformed token moves to a different grammar context.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All CSS errors discard complete stylesheet.
interactions.correctionRoute: Identify current parse unit and next recovery boundary.

interactions.title: Retrieve loading proof
interactions.learnerAction: List the minimum evidence proving an external stylesheet relationship resolved, returned CSS, parsed, matched, computed, and survived changed route depth.
interactions.artifactChange: External loading proof checklist is reconstructed.

evidence.observable: Proof covers relationship, resolved URL, response identity, parsed rule, match, computation, and changed path.
evidence.changedCase: Document moves into nested directory and receives a redirect.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Filename presence and successful status are complete load evidence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Follow resource from document metadata through browser computation.

interactions.title: Explain application-method change
interactions.learnerAction: Explain when a shared external stylesheet, bounded internal block, or inline declaration becomes appropriate after ownership and reuse constraints change.
interactions.artifactChange: Changed-constraint method explanation is recorded.

evidence.observable: Method choice changes only when reuse, ownership, update, delivery, or order evidence changes.
evidence.changedCase: Three-page service becomes one exported document with a unique override.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One CSS application method is universally correct.
interactions.correctionRoute: Compare actual owners, consumers, delivery, and update surfaces.

interactions.title: Retrieve basic selector matches
interactions.learnerAction: Enumerate exact match sets for universal, type, class, and identifier selectors on an unfamiliar changed document tree.
interactions.artifactChange: Unfamiliar selector match table is completed.

evidence.observable: All and only literal matches are listed, including duplicated identifier and newly inserted elements.
evidence.changedCase: A new semantic type repeats a class and copies one ID.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selectors match intent inferred from names rather than document tokens.
interactions.correctionRoute: Evaluate each element's type, class tokens, and identifier literally.

interactions.title: Correct universal and ID overuse
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a proposal that resets every element and gives every styled item a unique identifier, replacing it with proportional hooks.
interactions.artifactChange: Overcoupled selector plan is repaired.

evidence.observable: Repair narrows broad reach, uses reusable class membership, preserves unique identity, and retains native control states.
evidence.changedCase: A new native control and three repeated cards enter the page.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Universal reach and unique identifiers always reduce selector ambiguity.
interactions.correctionRoute: Derive exact current and future match sets from requirement reuse.

interactions.title: Correct integrated first-CSS trace
interactions.learnerAction: Correct a mixed trace containing semantic loss, incomplete document, wrong keyboard assumption, unsaved rule, malformed selector, wrong response, and overbroad hook.
interactions.artifactChange: Integrated correction and delayed-review schedule are saved.

evidence.observable: Correction orders defects by dependency, repairs minimally, retests all retained invariants, and completes a parallel trace.
evidence.changedCase: Defect combination changes order and produces a similar preview.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One CSS rewrite is faster and proves every earlier defect resolved.
interactions.correctionRoute: Repair earliest evidence divergence first and rerun downstream observations after each change.

activityDesigns.cumulativeArtifact: A no-hint performance bank samples independent classification, source inspection, coding, debugging, changed cases, and defense across the complete first-CSS competency boundary.
activityDesigns.startingState: Learner receives secure parallel HTML, CSS, network, parser, selector, computed, keyboard, and persistence fixtures unrelated to all prior scenarios.
activityDesigns.completionState: Learner submits fifteen independently graded evidence responses; misses route later to corrective instruction and a different reassessment form without exposing canonical answers.
activityDesigns.feedbackContract: During assessment, feedback records only submission and observable check result; diagnostic explanation appears after form closure and never exposes reusable canonical answers.
activityDesigns.hintContract: No instructional hints, syntax completions, answer elimination, or canonical source are available during any assessment interaction.
activityDesigns.correctionContract: A failed concept routes after form closure to targeted worked contrast, changed practice, and a secure parallel reassessment with distinct source and evidence.

interactions.title: Classify changed web requirements
interactions.learnerAction: Classify unfamiliar requirements for meaning, complete document metadata, presentation, keyboard task order, dynamic behavior, and user preference.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure layer-classification response is submitted.

evidence.observable: Every requirement is assigned to responsible layer and required meaning survives unavailable author CSS.
evidence.changedCase: A visual badge becomes required status and a user changes text settings.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible requirements all belong to CSS.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction compares meaning and behavior with author styles disabled.

interactions.title: Repair complete document envelope
interactions.learnerAction: Repair an unfamiliar document so its doctype, root, language, head metadata, title, stylesheet relationship, and body content have correct ownership.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure complete-document repair is submitted.

evidence.observable: Repaired source is complete and continues resolving intended CSS after document path changes.
evidence.changedCase: Document is relocated under a nested route.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Rendered body output is enough to prove complete source.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction inspects serialized envelope and metadata consumers.

interactions.title: Defend logical task order
interactions.learnerAction: Defend source and keyboard order for an unfamiliar styled task without using visual order or positive tabindex as evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure task-order defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense cites source, reading, and focus sequence across styled and unstyled states.
evidence.changedCase: One action becomes visually prominent while remaining late in task dependencies.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visual prominence proves focus priority.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction traverses source and focus independently.

interactions.title: Select falsifying evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Select the first evidence surface that can falsify six unfamiliar HTML, load, parser, match, computed, and persistence hypotheses.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure diagnostic selections are submitted.

evidence.observable: Selections match each claim to direct consumer evidence rather than appearance.
evidence.changedCase: Hypotheses are paired to produce overlapping symptoms.

interactions.feedbackTarget: One validator or screenshot can falsify every hypothesis.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction maps artifacts to their actual consumers.

interactions.title: Order first-CSS evidence
interactions.learnerAction: Order unfamiliar saved-source, parsed-rule, selector-match, declaration, computed-value, rendered-output, checkpoint, and reload observations.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure pipeline ordering response is submitted.

evidence.observable: Evidence is ordered by dependency and each stage has a bounded proof claim.
evidence.changedCase: Rendered output stays constant while source and match evidence change.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Visible output order determines source pipeline order.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction reconstructs browser and persistence dependencies.

interactions.title: Debug reload-only regression
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose and repair an unfamiliar style that works before reload but disappears afterward using durable source and browser evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure persistence diagnosis and repair are submitted.

evidence.observable: Repair reconciles buffer, saved file, served source, match, computed result, and reload.
evidence.changedCase: A second transient edit affects another declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Reload regression necessarily means unsupported CSS.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction compares transient and durable artifacts.

interactions.title: Defend HTML and CSS boundary
interactions.learnerAction: Defend which unfamiliar artifact facts remain semantic HTML and which presentation changes belong in CSS across disabled styles and user preferences.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure boundary defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense preserves required meaning and operation without requiring identical author appearance.
evidence.changedCase: Author colors disappear and user text size doubles.

interactions.feedbackTarget: CSS owns all visible distinctions and browser defaults are irrelevant.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction compares unstyled meaning and user-controlled presentation.

interactions.title: Label unfamiliar rule anatomy
interactions.learnerAction: Label selector, qualified rule, declaration block, declarations, properties, values, terminators, comment, and at-rule in secure mixed source.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure anatomy labels are submitted.

evidence.observable: Labels accurately distinguish nested syntax roles and rule categories.
evidence.changedCase: Source changes from statement at-rule to block at-rule and adds a declaration.

interactions.feedbackTarget: All CSS blocks are selector-led style rules.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction identifies at-keyword and qualified-rule boundaries.

interactions.title: Debug two parser scopes
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose one invalid selector and one invalid declaration value, repair minimally, and prove unrelated rules and sibling declarations survive.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure parser diagnoses and repairs are submitted.

evidence.observable: Rule-level and declaration-level recovery are distinguished through parsed and computed evidence.
evidence.changedCase: Malformed tokens move to new rules with different match counts.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Whole stylesheet replacement is required after any syntax error.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction locates current parse unit and first divergence.

interactions.title: Choose CSS application method
interactions.learnerAction: Choose external, internal, or inline CSS for an unfamiliar changed ownership and reuse brief, then state evidence that would verify it.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure application decision is submitted.

evidence.observable: Method follows actual reuse, cache, ownership, update, order, and verification constraints.
evidence.changedCase: Single document becomes four pages with one shared presentation system.

interactions.feedbackTarget: External CSS is always correct without changed constraints.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction models owners, consumers, and delivery.

interactions.title: Debug misleading stylesheet response
interactions.learnerAction: Diagnose a stylesheet request that returns successful HTML, restore correct resource identity, and prove parsed rule and computed value return.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure load diagnosis and repair are submitted.

evidence.observable: Final URL, media type, body, parsed rule, match, and computation prove cause and repair.
evidence.changedCase: HTML response arrives through a redirect with status 200.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Successful status means selector logic is at fault.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction inspects resource identity before match.

interactions.title: Predict four selector sets
interactions.learnerAction: Predict exact universal, type, class, and identifier match sets for secure changed markup containing insertion and duplicate identity.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure selector-set prediction is submitted.

evidence.observable: Prediction lists every literal match and identifies accidental broad or duplicate reach.
evidence.changedCase: A new semantic type repeats a class and copies an identifier.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Selector names guarantee intended membership.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction evaluates changed tree token by token.

interactions.title: Implement proportional selector hooks
interactions.learnerAction: Implement type, class, and unique identifier hooks for an unfamiliar brief without universal reset, semantic replacement, or duplicated identity.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure selector implementation is submitted.

evidence.observable: All intended elements match, unrelated elements do not, repeated roles use classes, and unique target remains unique.
evidence.changedCase: Another component and new native control are inserted.

interactions.feedbackTarget: IDs on every styled element provide safest precision.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction derives hooks from reuse and identity.

interactions.title: Debug selector overreach
interactions.learnerAction: Repair an unfamiliar universal reset, overbroad type selector, missing class token, and duplicated identifier without changing semantic source order.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure selector-overreach repair is submitted.

evidence.observable: Repair restores exact intended sets, native control states, unique identity, and logical order.
evidence.changedCase: A new control and repeated component enter repaired page.

interactions.feedbackTarget: Replacing every selector with IDs is a valid repair.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction separates broad, reusable, and unique membership.

interactions.title: Defend complete first-CSS solution
interactions.learnerAction: Defend an unfamiliar complete first-stylesheet solution using semantic HTML, complete source, logical order, targeted diagnostics, persistence, parser, loading, selector, and changed-case evidence.
interactions.artifactChange: Secure integrated performance defense is submitted.

evidence.observable: Defense connects every claim to direct current and changed evidence, rejected alternatives, limits, and retained tasks.
evidence.changedCase: Document moves, content grows, one class repeats, identifier duplicates, and author styles become unavailable.

interactions.feedbackTarget: A polished screenshot and valid syntax prove complete first-CSS competence.
interactions.correctionRoute: Post-form correction rebuilds evidence chain and parallel reassessment.

authorshipBoundary.interpretation: This artifact fixes interaction intent and evidence before authoring; it is neither publishable learner content nor a source for automatic prose generation.

gaps: Independent CSS subject, instructional-design, assessment, accessibility, responsive, assistive-technology, browser-interoperability, and duplication reviews remain incomplete.

Learner-facing explanations, source fixtures, original starters, canonical answers, executable parser and network fixtures, changed-case checks, feedback variants, persisted correction schedules, and learner pilots remain unauthored.
